title: Environment.sanitizeArguments only matches /usr/bin/xctest, breaks Swift Testing-based tests
author: acomley

title: Behavior change in Xcode 26.4 causes Vapor to not find .env files in unit test, rendering documentation invalid
author: marcomasser
body:
I posted about this on the Swift forums: [.env files are not found when running unit tests using Xcode 26.4](https://forums.swift.org/t/env-files-are-not-found-when-running-unit-tests-using-xcode-26-4/86043)

To summarize: Starting in Xcode 26.4, unit tests run with a working directory of `/tmp` instead of the one configured in the _Run_ action, so .env files are not found anymore. But that is the exact approach recommended in the Vapor documentation here: https://docs.vapor.codes/getting-started/xcode/

Is there any guideline on how to best deal with this in Vapor or a new recommendation for the project setup? At the very least, the documentation should probably be updated. Please let me know if this is the wrong repository to post about that.

title: There are semantic issues during migration.
author: Muning69
body:
When you want a unique field：
`.unique(on: .email, .username)`
In fact, it is a unique combination.
This is easy to misunderstand.
It doesn't quite conform to Swift's syntax.
It is more suitable for representing that all characters are unique, rather than combinations.

I suggest that the combination should have a significant parameter.
such as: `.unique(on: .email, .username, type: .compose)`
Clear identification of the combination is required, otherwise it will be considered unique.
The example is not a really good look, but the original writing is very confusing and does not conform to Swift syntax.

title: Only the first cookie is received over HTTP/2
author: lmcd
body:
### Describe the issue

Vapor only assumes one cookie header will be present. But over HTTP/2 on Safari, each cookie is delivered as a separate header line. All headers except the first are thrown away.

### Vapor version

4.121.2

### Operating system and version

macOS

### Swift version

Swift Package Manager - Swift 6.2.0-dev

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Outcome

_No response_

### Additional notes

This seems to be quite a critical bug 🚨 Could somebody validate?

Reading the incoming headers in `HTTPServerRequestDecoder.channelRead`, I can see headers are being received by the browser one by one as separate lines.

This appears to be due to legacy HTTP/1 assumptions whereby headers arrive like this:

`Cookie: a=1; b=2; c=3`

Over HTTP/2, they're arriving into `channelRead` like this:

```
cookie: a=1
cookie: b=2
cookie: c=3
```

The root of the problem seems to be here in `HTTPCookies.swift`:

    let cookies = parsed.first.map {
        HTTPCookies(directives: $0)
    }

It seems to me that instead of taking the first result, we should instead be merging them.

I've debugged this section, and I can see that `parsed` does indeed contain all of the cookies provided by the browser, but in the end this is resolved down to just one cookie.

title: Vapor Validations
author: oprisk
body:
### Describe the issue

Configuring Vapor's global JSON Content encoder/decoder (ISO8601 dates) breaks Vapor.Validations

### Vapor version

4.117.0

### Operating system and version

Mac OSX Tahoe v26

### Swift version

Swift Package Manager - Swift 6.2.0

### Steps to reproduce

1. Validation breaks by modifying Vapor's global encoder/decoder
2. Procedure to reproduce:
3. modify the App configuration.swift to include:
4. // Configure Vapor's global JSON Content encoder/decoder (ISO8601 dates)
5. var contentConfig = ContentConfiguration()
    contentConfig.use(encoder: DateCoding.encoder, for: .json)
    contentConfig.use(decoder: DateCoding.decoder, for: .json)
    ContentConfiguration.global = contentConfig
    ContentConfiguration.global.use(encoder: CustomEncoder(), for: .json)
    ContentConfiguration.global.use(decoder: CustomDecoder(), for: .json)
    
 omitting the contentConfig Vapor.Validations vapor.Validations works just fine.
 including contentConfig breaks Validations with the following error:
Signup failed with status: 400
{
  "reason": "Data corrupted at path ''. Validation executor couldn't find any validations to run (broken Decoder?).",
  "error": true
}
see Attachments:




### Outcome

Signup failed with status: 400
{
  "reason": "Data corrupted at path ''. Validation executor couldn't find any validations to run (broken Decoder?).",
  "error": true
}

### Additional notes

[Vapor.Validation.Example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24941379/Vapor.Validation.Example.zip)

title: FileMiddleware Hashing should be streaming
author: 0xTim
body:
### Please summarize your feature request

Don't load the whole file into memory

### Describe the functionality you're seeking in more detail

For calculating the hashes of files for etags, we currently load the whole file into memory. This is obviously inefficient (see #3405) and Crypto actually offers `update()` functions to do streaming hashing. We should use that

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

title: Empty response bodies are compressed by Vapor
author: semicoleon
body:
### Describe the issue

Requests with an Accept-Encoding header supported by Vapor cause empty body responses to be "compressed" from 0 to 8 bytes with deflate

### Vapor version

4.120.0

### Operating system and version

macOS 15.5

### Swift version

Swift Package Manager - Swift 6.1.1

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone [the demo repo](https://github.com/semicoleon/vapor-response-compression-bug).
2. Run the example test case, which will fail.

### Outcome

An empty response body (and maybe even any sufficiently small body) should not be compressed automatically by Vapor, but it currently is.

### Additional notes

As I was digging into this issue (which was found by a vendor calling our APIs and failing to decompress the 8 bytes the empty body was encoded into) I noticed that Vapor is using a compression predicate that checks a blacklist of `Content-Type`s to decide whether to compress, but this response doesn't have a `Content-Type` (because it's empty) so that code just passes.

But it not crash everytime. ### Outcome _No response_ ### Additional notes _No response_

title: It appears a head is missing in 206 Partial Content.
author: iwecon
body:
in Vapor (version: 4.119.2):
`vapor/Utilities/FileIO.swift:185`
`vapor/Utilities/FileIO.swift:544`

Codes: 
```swift
// line 185:
box.headers.add(name: .accept, value: contentRange.unit.serialize())

// line 544:
response.headers.add(name: .accept, value: contentRange.unit.serialize())
```

Description:
Only `Accept` header.
The `Accept-Ranges` header is missing.


When I use `Cloudflare` to proxy my `Vapor HTTP service`, without `Accept-Ranges`, Cloudflare cannot recognize it as a 206 response and always returns a 200 response, meaning it does not support resuming interrupted downloads.

I tried adding Accept-Ranges to Nginx, and then Cloudflare returned a 206 response correctly.

I'm not sure if this is a bug; I don't know much about HTTP headers.

I'm just pointing out this issue.

title: Multi type routing HTTP methods
author: Muning69
body:
### Please summarize your feature request

The same route should support multiple HTTP methods, and Swift provides a syntax for variadic parameters that can be used to implement Vapor routing.

### Describe the functionality you're seeking in more detail

Old: `app.on(.GET, "profile", use: profile)`
New: `app.on(.HEAD, .GET, "profile", use: profile)`
New: `app.on(.PATCH, .PUT, "profile", use: profile)`
New: `app.on(.all, "profile", use: profile)`

Example: `app.on(HTTPMethod..., PathComponent..., use: (Request) -> Response)`

Multiple HTTP methods can be supported simultaneously using a single route for requests.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

title: Macro Based Route Groups
author: 0xTim
body:
### Please summarize your feature request

Allow Route Groups to be defined with Macros to attached to routes

### Describe the functionality you're seeking in more detail

We should support route groups as macros to make it easy to support attaching routes to route groups. This will only be for path components - middleware is handled in #3396

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

title: Macro Based Middleware

### Have you considered any alternatives?
